task: I need to send a firm written complaint about a damaged item and make sure I’ve got my facts straight before I mail anything. Can you help me draft something that’s clear, organized, and hard to ignore, with a version I could keep for my records too?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. I’d do it in two parts: a **clean complaint letter** you can mail, and a **record copy** with the facts and attachments noted.

## What to include
Keep it tight and factual:
- What the item was
- When and where you got it
- What damage/problem you found
- Why you’re contacting them
- What you want them to do
- A deadline for response
- Your contact info and account/order number if relevant

## Draft complaint letter
You can copy this and fill in the blanks:

---

**[Your Name]**  
**[Your Address]**  
**[City, State ZIP]**  
**[Email / Phone]**  
**[Date]**

**[Company Name]**  
**[Company Address]**  
**[City, State ZIP]**

**Re: Damaged Item / Order #[___] / Account #[___]**

Dear [Company Name],

I am writing to formally complain about a damaged item I received from your company.

On **[date received]**, I received **[item name/description]** from **[order number, invoice number, or account number]**. When I opened/inspected it, I found **[brief description of the damage]**. This item was **[new / unused / expected to be in good condition]**, and the damage was not disclosed at the time of purchase.

I am requesting **[replacement / refund / repair / account credit]**. Please confirm in writing how you will resolve this matter.

For your reference, I have included/attached **[photos, receipt, delivery record, correspondence, etc.]**.

Please respond by **[date — usually 10 to 14 days from mailing, or a specific deadline if needed]**.

Sincerely,  
**[Your Name]**

---

## Record copy to keep
I’d keep a version for your file that includes a little more detail:

**Complaint Record Copy**

- **Date sent:** [date]
- **Sent to:** [company name and mailing address]
- **Method:** [mail / certified mail / email]
- **Item:** [full description]
- **Order / invoice / account number:** [number]
- **Date received:** [date]
- **Damage observed:** [short factual description]
- **Requested resolution:** [refund / replacement / repair / credit]
- **Response deadline requested:** [date]
- **Attachments included:** [photos, receipt, shipping label, etc.]

**Notes:**  
[Anything else important — who you spoke with, prior attempts, tracking number, etc.]

## A few practical tips
- Keep the tone firm, not emotional.
- Stick to facts you can prove.
- If it matters, send it **certified mail with return receipt** and keep a copy of everything.
- Take photos of the item and packaging before you mail anything.
- Don’t send originals unless they specifically ask.

If you want, I can turn this into a **more forceful version** or a **shorter version for email**.

user_simulator.text:
That’s the right shape, but it’s still generic. I’d want it a little tighter and more specific about the facts, because vague letters get ignored.

I need a version with:
- a harder opening line
- a clear statement that the item was damaged on arrival or found damaged after delivery
- a demand for one specific remedy, not three choices if I already know what I want
- a line that says I’m keeping copies and expect a written response
- a separate record copy that’s more like a log entry, with dates and attachments listed cleanly

Also, if this is going by mail, I’d want wording that doesn’t sound chatty. Plain and firm. No extra fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. Here’s a tighter, firmer version you can use by mail, plus a clean record copy for your files.

## Mail version

**[Your Name]**  
**[Your Address]**  
**[City, State ZIP]**  
**[Email / Phone]**  
**[Date]**

**[Company Name]**  
**[Company Address]**  
**[City, State ZIP]**

**Re: Damaged item received — Order #[___] / Account #[___]**

Dear [Company Name]:

I am writing to notify you that the item I received was damaged on arrival / was found damaged after delivery.

On **[date received]**, I received **[item name and description]** under **[order/invoice/account number]**. After delivery, I found **[brief, specific description of the damage]**. The item was not in the condition expected at delivery.

I am requesting a **full refund** for this item. Please confirm in writing how and when this will be resolved.

I am keeping copies of this letter and all supporting records. I expect a written response by **[date]**.

Sincerely,  
**[Your Name]**

---

## Record copy for your files

**Complaint log entry**

- **Date prepared:** [date]
- **Date sent:** [date]
- **Sent to:** [company name, mailing address]
- **Method:** [first-class mail / certified mail / email]
- **Item:** [full item description]
- **Order / invoice / account number:** [number]
- **Date received:** [date]
- **Condition issue:** [brief factual description of damage]
- **Requested remedy:** Full refund
- **Response deadline requested:** [date]
- **Attachments included:**  
  - [photo of damage]  
  - [photo of packaging]  
  - [receipt / invoice]  
  - [delivery confirmation]  
  - [other supporting documents]

**Notes:**  
[Tracking number, customer service contact, prior messages, or anything else relevant]

If you want, I can also make this into a **more formal certified-mail version** or a **shorter version with stronger legal-style wording**.

user_simulator.text:
Better. That’s usable.

The opening is firm enough, and the refund demand is clean. I’d still want one small thing tightened: don’t say “damaged on arrival / was found damaged after delivery” with the slash in the final copy. Pick one and state it straight, since that looks sloppy if I’m mailing it.

The record copy is fine. Clean enough to file.

If you’re going to revise it one more time, make the opening a little harder — something like a plain notice that the item was received damaged and that I’m disputing the condition at delivery. No chatter, no softening.
